sections.instruction: Questions 1-3: Complete the form below. Write ONE WORD OR A NUMBER. Questions 4-8: Complete the table below. Write ONE WORD OR A NUMBER. Questions 9 and 10: Choose TWO letters, A-E.
sections.passage_or_context:
Rented Properties Customer’s Requirements
Name: Steven Godfrey
No. of bedrooms: 4
Preferred location: in the (1) {{BLANK}} area of town
Maximum monthly rent: (2) £ {{BLANK}}
Length of let required: (3) {{BLANK}}
Starting: September 1st

questions.content: Which TWO facilities in the district of Devon Close are open to the public at the moment?

sections.instruction: Questions 11-16: Complete the notes below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS OR A NUMBER. Questions 17-20: Complete the table below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS OR A NUMBER.
Questions 21-26: Choose the correct letter A, B or C. Questions 27-30: Write the correct letter A, B or C next to questions 27-30.

questions.content: Paul decided to get work experience in South America because he wanted
options: C to learn about Latin American life

questions.content: What project work did Paul originally intend to get involved in?
Why did Paul change from one project to another?
options: A His first job was not well organized
B He found doing the routine work very boring
C The work was too physically demanding

questions.content: In the village community, he learnt how important it was to
What does Paul say about his project manager?
options: A He let Paul do most of the work
B His plans were too ambitious
C He was very supportive of Paul

questions.content: Paul was surprised to be given
options: B so little money to live on
C an extension to his contract

sections.instruction: Questions 31-34: Choose the correct letter A, B or C. Questions 35-40: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY.

questions.content: Compared to introducing new business processes, attempts to copy existing processes are
Most research into the repetition of success in business has
options: A been done outside the United States
C related to only a few contexts

questions.content: What does the speaker say about consulting experts?
options: A Too few managers ever do it
B It can be useful in certain circumstances
C Experts are sometimes unwilling to give advice

questions.content: An expert’s knowledge about a business system may be incomplete because
options: A some details are difficult for workers to explain
B workers choose not to mention certain details
C details are sometimes altered by workers

questions.content:
Setting up systems based on an existing process
Two mistakes:
Manager tries to:
• Improve on the original process
• Create an ideal (35) {{BLANK}} from the best parts of several processes
Cause of problems
• Information was inaccurate
• Comparison between the business settings was invalid
• Disadvantages were overlooked e.g. effect of changes on (36) {{BLANK}}
Solution
• Change (37) {{BLANK}}
• Impose rigorous (38) {{BLANK}}
• Copy original very closely:
o Physical features of the (39) {{BLANK}}
o The (40) {{BLANK}} of original employees
